description: Breaking Fantasy Football player news along with the impact for fantasy managers

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Dolphins placed Darrell Baker Jr. on the PUP list.
items.impact: Baker won't be ready to go for the start of training camp. He can be activated at any time.
items.title: Darrell Baker Jr. placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Dolphins placed Storm Duck on the PUP list.
items.impact: Duck missed the end of last season with a knee injury. He can be activated at any time during training camp.
items.title: Storm Duck (knee) placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Browns say Quinshon Judkins will be a full-go at the start of training camp.
items.impact: Judkins was able to practice during OTAs. He is working his way back from a fractured fibula and a broken ankle. He's in line to remain in the lead role in the Browns backfield.
items.title: Quinshon Judkins (ankle/fibula) to be full-go at training camp

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: The Giants have placed defensive lineman Sam Roberts on the physically unable to perform list.
items.impact: Roberts is recovering from an undisclosed off-season surgery, but is expected to participate in training camp at some point. Players are eligible to come off the PUP list and practice immediately at any point during camp.
items.title: Sam Roberts placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Cardinals coach Mike LaFleur said QB Jacoby Brissett will practice at training camp on Thursday.
items.impact: Brissett will officially not be a hold-in as he continues to seek a new contract.
items.title: Jacoby Brissett to practice in training camp Thursday

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: The Giants have placed defensive tackle Roy Robertson-Harris (Achilles) on the physically unable to perform list.
items.impact: Robertson-Harris suffered a torn right Achillies at OTA's in May, and will miss most of, if not all of the 2026 season.
items.title: Roy Robertson-Harris (Achilles) placed on PUP list

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: The New York Giants have placed tight end Thomas Fidone II (foot) on the physically unable to perform list ahead of training camp.
items.impact: Fidone underwent a minor cleanup procedure on his foot in the spring, and head coach John Harbaugh said the recovery would require about two weeks. While Fidone begins camp on the PUP list, he likely won't be there long as players are eligible to come off the list at any point during camp.
items.title: Thomas Fidone II (foot) placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Malik Nabers is expected to avoid being placed on the PUP list to begin training camp.
items.impact: Nabers is continuing to work his way back from a torn ACL. In June, Giants coach John Harbaugh estimated that Nabers was about 80 percent of the way through his rehab schedule. His status will need to continue to be monitored over the next month and a half into the start of the season.
items.title: Malik Nabers (knee) expected to avoid PUP list to begin training camp

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: Cardinals tight end Tip Reiman has been placed on the physically unable to perform list ahead of training camp.
items.impact: Reiman went on season-ending injured reserve after injuring his ankle in a Week 5 against the Titans, and will begin training camp on the PUP list. However, players can come off of the PUP list at any point during camp, increasing Reiman's chances at getting some work in ahead of the regular season.
items.title: Tip Reiman (ankle) placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Titans signed OT Zachary Thomas.
items.impact: Thomas was recently released by the 49ers. He will compete for a depth role on the Titans offensive line.
items.title: Zachary Thomas signs with Titans

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Cardinals did not place Trey Benson on the PUP list.
items.impact: Benson missed time earlier in the Cardinals offseason program due to an ankle injury. His role in the Cardinals backfield remains uncertain.
items.title: Trey Benson (ankle) avoids PUP list

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: The Cardinals have placed cornerback Garrett Williams (Achilles) on the physically unable to perform list.
items.impact: The move isn't totally unexpected, as Williams suffered a season-ending torn Achilles in a Week 16 game against the Falcons last season. However, the expectation is that Williams will be ready to begin the regular season on time.
items.title: Garrett Williams (Achilles) placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: James Conner avoided being placed on the PUP list.
items.impact: Conner had missed time earlier in the Cardinals offseason program recovering from an ankle injury. His role in the Cardinals backfield remains up in the air heading into the season following the offseason additions of Jeremiyah Love and Tyler Allgeier.
items.title: James Conner (ankle) avoids PUP list

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: The Cardinals have placed defensive end Josh Sweat on the physically unable to perform list ahead of training camp.
items.impact: The Cardinals didn't disclose the nature of Sweat's ailment, but it is likely minor, as players can come off the PUP list at any point during training camp. The 29-year-old suited up for all 17 regular-season games last season, his first with the Cardinals.
items.title: Josh Sweat placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Patriots hosted Jeremiah Franklin for a workout.
items.impact: Franklin went undrafted out of Boston College. He'll look to earn a roster spot with New England in training camp.
items.title: Jeremiah Franklin works out for Patriots

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: The Cardinals have placed tackle Paris Johnson Jr. on the physically unable to perform list ahead of training camp.
items.impact: The Cardinals didn't announced why Johnson is hitting the PUP list, but he did miss the final four games of last season due to a left MCL sprain. The 25-year-old has missed eight games over the last two seasons after suiting up for all 17 games as a rookie in the 2023 campaign.
items.title: Paris Johnson Jr. placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Browns placed Maliek Collins on the PUP list.
items.impact: Collins suffered a season-ending quad injury in Week 13 of last season. He can be activated at any time during training camp.
items.title: Maliek Collins (quad) placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Eagles hosted LB Chandler Martin for a visit on Thursday.
items.impact: Martin was with the Eagles earlier this offseason but he was waived on June 10. He is seeking a spot in training camp.
items.title: Chandler Martin visits Eagles Thursday

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: Cardinals defensive lineman Walter Nolen III (knee) is healthy and will avoid the physically unable to perform list entering training camp.
items.impact: Nolen suffered a season-ending left knee injury on Dec. 21, one that required successful surgery. The 2025 16th overall pick was able to suit up for just six games in his rookie season as a result.
items.title: Walter Nolen III (knee) healthy entering camp

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: Titans tackle JC Latham (hip) has been placed on the physically unable to perform list ahead of camp.
items.impact: Reports have indicated that the hip issue that Latham is dealing with is not serious, and he is eligible to come off the PUP list at any time during training camp. The 2024 seventh-overall pick appeared in all 17 games in his rookie season but missed four games due to injury a season ago.
items.title: JC Latham (hip) placed on PUP list

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: Titans safety Amani Hooker has been placed on the physically unable to perform list prior to training camp.
items.impact: Hooker is dealing with an undisclosed ailment, but doesn't appear to be in jeopardy of missing and regaulr-season games. The seven-year veteran suited up for 16 games last season, recording 81 total tackles, three of which were for a loss.
items.title: Amani Hooker (undisclosed) placed on PUP list

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: The Ravens have waived quarterback Diego Pavia.
items.impact: The Ravens signed the undrafted quarterback out of Vanderbilt to a three-year deal in April, but have waived him prior to training camp. The 24-year-old should garner some interest on the open market for teams seeking depth in their quarterback room.
items.title: Diego Pavia waived by Ravens

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.desc: Nic Scourton has been diagnosed with a torn ACL. He will get a second opinion to confirm it, and if it's confirmed, will have surgery in the next few weeks. His 2026 is all but over.
items.impact: It's a brutal injury to have happen at any time, but especially when it happens within minutes of starting the first day of practice. He will look to rest and recover for next year, and hopefully we will see his breakout in 2027.
items.title: Nic Scourton diagnosed with torn-ACL

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Jack Plummer is scheduled to work out for the 49ers and Dolphins.
items.impact: Plummer was the MVP in the UFL. He is scheduled to workout for the 49ers on Friday and the Dolphins next week.
items.title: Jack Plummer working out for 49ers, Dolphins

items.author: Diego Sandoval
items.desc: Fernando Mendoza signed his 4-year, $57.27 million rookie deal with the Las Vegas Raiders on Thursday.
items.impact: Mendoza's first NFL contract is fully guaranteed and includes a fifth-year option. He will head into training camp in a positional battle with Kirk Cousins for who will be under center for the Raiders in Week 1.
items.title: Fernando Mendoza signs rookie contract with Raiders

title: Fantasy Player News
